name: Telegram Mini App household and invite flows
description: Bring dedicated household list/detail surfaces to TMA first, including household avatar setup/editing and home-screen entry points, while preserving worker-owned membership and role rules. Invite preview/accept and `startapp` deep-link behavior stay in the broader feature but are not part of this execution pass.

planned: Add additive `household.avatarUrl` persistence + API support in the worker without changing existing household semantics.
Build `/households` and `/households/:id` TMA routes with Telegram-native list/detail layout and direct links from the home surface.
Preserve worker-owned household role and membership rules; admin-only actions stay enforced server-side and reflected in TMA affordances.
artifacts: Added worker migration `0002_household_avatar.sql` plus household contract/repository/handler updates so `HouseholdDTO` now returns `avatarUrl` on create/list/detail/update and `PATCH /api/v1/households/:id` accepts additive `avatarUrl` updates.

Added TMA route constants in `apps/tma/src/lib/constants/routes.ts`, a shared authenticated API client in `apps/tma/src/lib/api/client.ts`, and local media helpers in `apps/tma/src/lib/media/*` so household pages can reuse typed requests and signed Cloudinary uploads without importing `apps/web` code.

Built `apps/tma/src/features/households/*` plus new `/households` and `/households/:id` routes with live worker-backed list/detail UI, admin avatar/name editing, current-month summary, recent household expenses, member list, and create-household entry from the list page.
Updated the TMA home route and shell to use centralized path constants, enabled the `Gia đình` shortcut, and turned home household cards into direct links to the new detail route.
Expanded local seed data so the Telegram test user's two demo households now carry seeded `avatar_url` values for immediate visual verification on home/list/detail screens.
verification: Focused worker verification passed: `pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/unit/dto-household.spec.ts test/integration/households-read-update.spec.ts` succeeded with 18 tests after the avatar contract/persistence change.

Focused TMA verification passed: `pnpm --filter tma typecheck` succeeded and `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/home-presentation.test.ts src/test/household-presentation.test.ts` passed with 6 tests.
Focused TMA build verification passed: `pnpm --filter tma build` succeeded. Vite emitted a >500kB chunk-size warning only; the production build still completed successfully.

Local data verification passed: `pnpm --filter worker db:migrate:local` applied `0002_household_avatar.sql`, `pnpm --filter worker db:seed:local` succeeded, and a follow-up local D1 query confirmed the Telegram test account `01KT3YMJ8GHFQD6K0RM4FJEJT4` now sees `Demo Household` and `City Loft` with non-null `avatar_url` values.

Repo-level verification passed after removing one stale unused dependency line from `apps/tma/package.json`: touched harness JSON parse checks printed `OK`, `./scripts/check_harness_size.sh` printed `Harness size checks passed`, `git diff --check` was clean, `./init.sh build` printed `OK`, and final `./init.sh` completed with `Done!`.

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: all, repo: household-finance-system)` returned `critical` with 25 changed files and 20 affected processes because the active worktree also contains other TMA route/shell edits outside the strict household slice. The result should be read as whole-worktree blast radius, not as an isolated household-avatar risk signal.

notes: This feature is the main TMA household/onboarding slice. The current pass is intentionally narrowed to list/detail + avatar so it can land cleanly without mixing invite deep-link work into the same verification batch.
